news_data: HDFC Bank reported quarterly net profit growth of around 20%, supported by strong retail and corporate loan demand, highlighting resilience compared to state-run banks facing rising non-performing asset pressures. (Reuters)

HDFC Bank announced strong quarterly earnings with stable margins and low NPAs, reinforcing its conservative lending model while the broader Indian banking sector struggled with deteriorating asset quality.

Following demonetisation announced by the Indian government, HDFC Bank witnessed a surge in deposits and digital transactions as customers moved towards formal banking and electronic payment systems. (Wikipedia)
HDFC Bank reported steady growth driven by expansion in rural and semi-urban markets, focusing on retail lending and maintaining strong asset quality compared to peers facing rising stressed assets.

HDFC Bank delivered consistent quarterly performance with strong retail loan growth and stable margins, continuing to outperform peers in asset quality during a period of stress in the banking sector.
HDFC Bank posted strong earnings growth with stable asset quality and improving margins, driven by expansion in retail lending and disciplined risk management across its diversified loan portfolio.

HDFC Bank became India’s most valuable lender by market capitalization, reflecting strong investor confidence in its consistent profitability, governance standards, and long-term growth strategy in private banking.

HDFC Bank reported strong quarterly earnings supported by robust retail loan growth and stable asset quality, maintaining leadership position among private sector banks amid broader economic slowdown concerns.
HDFC Bank maintained steady profit growth and stable margins despite macroeconomic challenges, supported by strong retail lending and disciplined underwriting practices across its credit portfolio.

HDFC Bank continued expanding its digital banking services, focusing on improving customer experience and increasing transaction volumes through technology adoption and retail-focused growth initiatives.

HDFC Bank announced support measures during COVID-19 disruptions, including loan moratoriums and liquidity assistance, aiming to protect customers and maintain operational stability during economic uncertainty.

Despite pandemic-related disruptions, HDFC Bank reported resilient earnings and stable asset quality, demonstrating strong risk management and diversified lending across retail and corporate segments.

RBI barred HDFC Bank from issuing new credit cards after repeated digital outages, highlighting operational risks and forcing the bank to strengthen its technology infrastructure and compliance systems. (Reuters)

HDFC Bank initiated major investments in digital infrastructure and compliance improvements following RBI restrictions, aiming to restore operational stability and support future growth in digital banking services.

HDFC Bank invested in a payments entity promoted by Tata Group, marking strategic expansion into India’s digital payments ecosystem and strengthening its presence in evolving fintech infrastructure. (Wikipedia)

RBI imposed a monetary penalty on HDFC Bank for irregularities in its auto loan portfolio, highlighting compliance gaps and reinforcing regulatory scrutiny despite the bank’s otherwise strong financial performance. (Wikipedia)
HDFC Ltd announced a merger with HDFC Bank in India’s largest financial sector deal, aiming to combine housing finance and banking operations for scale, efficiency, and long-term growth. (Wikipedia)

HDFC Bank completed its merger with HDFC Ltd, creating one of the world’s largest banks with expanded balance sheet, increased customer base, and stronger retail and housing loan integration. (Wikipedia)

A data breach at HDB Financial Services, a subsidiary of HDFC Bank, exposed sensitive information of millions of customers, raising concerns around cybersecurity, data protection, and operational risk management. (Wikipedia)
RBI retained HDFC Bank in its list of domestic systemically important banks, requiring higher capital buffers and reinforcing its “too big to fail” status following the merger expansion. (Reuters)

HDFC Bank expanded its physical presence by opening new branches in underserved regions, strengthening distribution network and improving access to banking services across rural and semi-urban India. (Wikipedia)

HDB Financial Services, a subsidiary of HDFC Bank, filed for a $1.5 billion IPO to meet regulatory requirements, supporting capital expansion and improving transparency in its non-banking operations. (Reuters)

RBI imposed a penalty on HDFC Bank for non-compliance with deposit rate regulations and customer service standards, highlighting ongoing regulatory concerns despite its strong market leadership position. (Reuters)

HDFC Bank reported strong FY2025 results with rising net profit and net interest income, along with announcing a dividend, reflecting continued profitability despite post-merger integration challenges. (Republic World)
HDFC Bank scheduled board meeting to review quarterly results and consider dividend and bonus issuance, signaling continued focus on shareholder returns and capital allocation strategy. (mint)

Dubai regulator restricted a branch of HDFC Bank from onboarding new clients due to compliance deficiencies, raising concerns about international regulatory adherence and operational governance. (Reuters)
RBI imposed penalties on HDFC Bank for using incorrect loan pricing benchmarks and weak KYC outsourcing controls, highlighting lapses in compliance and need for stronger internal processes. (Reuters)
RBI approved HDFC Bank group entities to acquire up to 9.5% stake in IndusInd Bank, indicating strategic positioning and expansion within India’s financial services sector. (Reuters)
RBI introduced guidelines requiring banks to ringfence core and non-core operations, impacting large lenders like HDFC Bank and shaping future structure of financial conglomerates in India.

Post-merger integration challenges continued to affect HDFC Bank’s credit growth and margins, as the bank worked to align liquidity, systems, and lending strategies within the expanded balance sheet structure.
Reliance Jio launched beta 4G services for employees and partners, marking the beginning of its telecom entry and laying foundation for a nationwide digital transformation strategy. (Wikipedia)

Reliance Industries commercially launched Jio 4G services with free voice and data offers, disrupting India’s telecom sector and triggering intense competition through ultra-low pricing strategies. (Wikipedia)

Reliance Industries’ AGM highlighted aggressive telecom strategy and digital focus, with market reacting to Jio announcements as company positioned itself for long-term consumer technology leadership. (The Economic Times)

Reliance Jio crossed 100 million subscribers within months of launch, becoming fastest-growing telecom network globally and strengthening Reliance Industries’ position in India’s digital ecosystem. (Wikipedia)
Reliance Industries reported strong earnings growth supported by refining and petrochemical segments, while telecom business scaled rapidly with increasing subscriber additions and network expansion.

Reliance announced Jio GigaFiber broadband services, expanding into fixed-line internet and strengthening its integrated digital ecosystem alongside telecom, content, and enterprise connectivity offerings. (Wikipedia)
Reliance Industries posted strong quarterly earnings driven by refining margins and petrochemical performance, while telecom arm Jio continued rapid subscriber growth and revenue expansion.

Reliance Industries’ AGM announced major digital and retail expansion plans, including partnerships and platform strategy, signaling transition from energy-focused business to consumer and technology-driven conglomerate. (Reliance Industries Limited)

Reliance Industries announced acquisition of Fynd to strengthen e-commerce capabilities, expanding its retail digital strategy and integrating online platforms with physical retail network across India. (Wikipedia)
Reliance Industries continued to scale retail and telecom businesses, with strong growth in consumer segments contributing increasingly to overall revenue mix alongside traditional energy operations.
Reliance Industries became first Indian company to cross $200 billion market capitalization, driven by strong investor confidence in digital, telecom, and retail transformation strategy. (peeaar.in)

Reliance Industries reported resilient earnings despite COVID-19 disruptions, supported by telecom growth and stable refining operations, highlighting diversification benefits across business segments.
Reliance Industries announced strategic stake sale in Jio Platforms to global investors, raising significant capital and validating valuation of its digital business ecosystem.
Google announced investment in Jio Platforms, strengthening strategic partnership and supporting development of affordable smartphones and digital ecosystem expansion in India.

Reliance Retail attracted global investments to expand its footprint, signaling strong investor interest in India’s consumer market and supporting company’s transition towards digital commerce leadership.

Reliance Industries highlighted its strategy to become a global leader in digital services and new energy at its AGM, outlining investments in renewable energy and technology-driven growth initiatives. (Reliance Industries Limited)

Reliance Retail partnered with global convenience chain 7-Eleven to expand retail footprint in India, marking entry into organized convenience store segment and strengthening consumer-facing business strategy. (Wikipedia)

Reliance Retail invested approximately $200 million in Dunzo, acquiring a strategic stake to strengthen its last-mile delivery and hyperlocal commerce capabilities in India’s growing digital retail ecosystem. (Wikipedia)

At its AGM, Reliance Industries outlined plans for expansion into green energy, including solar manufacturing and hydrogen ecosystem, positioning itself as a major player in India’s clean energy transition. (Reliance Industries Limited)

Reliance Industries emphasized growth across telecom, retail, and new energy segments at its AGM, focusing on scaling digital platforms and strengthening integrated consumer and technology ecosystem. (Reliance Industries Limited)

Reliance announced demerger of its financial services business into Jio Financial Services, unlocking value and creating a separate listed entity focused on lending, insurance, and digital financial solutions.

Reliance Consumer Products relaunched Campa Cola brand to expand FMCG presence, aiming to compete with global beverage companies and strengthen its position in India’s fast-growing consumer goods market. (Wikipedia)

Reliance Industries and BP showcased energy transition initiatives at India Energy Week, focusing on fuel retail expansion and low-carbon solutions as part of broader shift towards sustainable energy business. (Reliance Industries Limited)

Reliance expanded digital infrastructure and telecom ecosystem through continued investments in 5G rollout and enterprise services, strengthening its leadership in India’s rapidly evolving digital economy.

At its AGM, Reliance Industries outlined strategy to become a deep-tech and advanced manufacturing company, focusing on AI, energy transition, and next-generation digital infrastructure investments. (Reliance Industries Limited)

Reliance Industries participated in India’s defence manufacturing initiative through Jai Ammunition Limited, supporting indigenous production of artillery systems and reducing dependence on imports. (Wikipedia)

Reliance Defence entered partnership with Germany’s Rheinmetall to manufacture ammunition in India, strengthening its presence in defence sector and expanding into strategic industrial manufacturing capabilities. (Wikipedia)

Reliance Defence secured export orders under global partnerships, marking expansion into international defence markets and reinforcing diversification beyond energy, telecom, and retail businesses. (Wikipedia)

At its AGM, Reliance announced plans to expand into artificial intelligence and digital products, including Jio platforms, while indicating potential IPO of telecom business to unlock shareholder value. (The Economic Times)

Reliance Industries highlighted strong performance despite global energy market volatility, emphasizing resilience of diversified business model and continued focus on growth across digital, retail, and energy segments. (The Economic Times)

INFY reported steady quarterly performance driven by large deal wins and stable client demand, while continuing its transition towards automation, digital services, and consulting-led revenue growth strategy.

INFY announced strong earnings growth with improving margins, supported by cost optimization and higher contribution from digital services, reflecting early success of its strategic transformation under new leadership.

INFY reported stable revenue growth amid global IT spending slowdown, focusing on automation and artificial intelligence to improve efficiency and maintain competitiveness in evolving technology services market.

INFY posted quarterly earnings with moderate growth, while increasing investments in digital transformation services, highlighting shift from traditional outsourcing toward high-value technology and consulting offerings.

INFY delivered steady earnings supported by digital services growth, though facing pricing pressure in traditional outsourcing segments as global clients increasingly shifted toward automation and cloud-based solutions.
INFY CEO Vishal Sikka resigned amid governance disputes with founders, triggering sharp stock decline and raising concerns over leadership stability and corporate governance practices. (Moneycontrol)

INFY appointed interim leadership following CEO resignation, aiming to stabilize operations and rebuild investor confidence while addressing governance concerns and internal conflicts between board and founders.
INFY appointed Salil Parekh as new CEO and managing director, signaling leadership reset and renewed focus on operational stability, client engagement, and sustainable growth strategy.

INFY under new CEO initiated strategic review, including plans to divest non-core acquisitions like Panaya and Skava, aiming to simplify operations and improve focus on core digital services business. (Moneycontrol)
INFY reported steady earnings recovery under new leadership, with improved client deal pipeline and focus on digital transformation services driving gradual improvement in revenue growth trajectory.

INFY delivered strong quarterly results supported by digital services growth and large deal wins, while maintaining stable margins and improving operational efficiency under renewed management direction.

INFY reported consistent revenue growth with strong performance in digital services segment, reflecting successful execution of strategy focused on cloud, automation, and next-generation technology solutions.

INFY posted strong quarterly earnings with rising deal wins and robust demand for digital services, reinforcing its position as a leading global IT services provider amid evolving technology landscape.
INFY announced measures to address COVID-19 disruptions, including cost controls and remote delivery models, ensuring business continuity while supporting clients during global economic uncertainty.

INFY reported resilient earnings despite pandemic challenges, driven by strong demand for digital transformation and cloud services as global enterprises accelerated technology adoption during crisis period.

INFY launched India’s new income tax e-filing portal, aiming to modernize tax administration, but users soon reported glitches, raising concerns over execution quality and government project delivery. (Wikipedia)

Finance Ministry summoned INFY CEO over persistent technical issues in the income tax portal, highlighting governance concerns and increasing scrutiny on large government technology projects executed by the company. (Wikipedia)

INFY reported strong deal wins and revenue growth, supported by global demand for digital transformation services, while continuing investments in cloud, automation, and enterprise technology solutions.

INFY posted steady quarterly earnings with strong large deal bookings, reflecting continued demand for digital services despite global uncertainties and strengthening its position in global IT services market.
INFY terminated employees involved in “moonlighting,” reinforcing strict employment policies and sparking industry-wide debate on dual employment practices in India’s IT services sector. (Wikipedia)

INFY reported strong quarterly performance driven by large deal wins and digital services growth, maintaining stable margins despite macroeconomic slowdown and cautious client spending environment globally.
INFY lowered revenue growth guidance citing weak global demand and slowdown in discretionary technology spending, reflecting challenges faced by IT sector amid economic uncertainty in key markets.

INFY continued to secure large deal wins despite slowdown concerns, demonstrating resilience in enterprise digital transformation demand and maintaining strong deal pipeline across global client segments.
INFY reported stable earnings but highlighted margin pressures due to pricing challenges and increased costs, reflecting ongoing adjustment phase in global IT demand and competitive environment.

INFY delivered quarterly earnings with moderate growth and cautious outlook, citing delays in client decision-making and reduced discretionary spending across major markets like the United States and Europe.

INFY reported 9% year-on-year profit growth with steady revenue increase, supported by large deal wins and operational efficiency, while maintaining cautious outlook amid uncertain global economic environment. (The Economic Times)
INFY announced quarterly results with continued deal momentum and stable margins, highlighting strong client demand for digital services and consistent execution across key business segments. (INFY)

INFY completed stake sale in its Japan-based joint venture HIPUS, strengthening strategic partnership with Mitsubishi Heavy Industries and focusing on optimizing global business structure. (The Times of India)

INFY promoter reduced stake through open market sale, reflecting portfolio rebalancing while company continued stable operations and maintained strong institutional investor participation. (scanx.trade)
INFY announced schedule for quarterly earnings release, reflecting continued transparency and structured investor communication as part of its governance and reporting framework. (INFY)

TCS became India’s most profitable company, surpassing Reliance Industries, reflecting strong earnings growth, operational efficiency, and global leadership in IT services and outsourcing business. (Wikipedia)
A US jury imposed a $940 million fine on TCS in a trade secrets lawsuit involving Epic Systems, raising concerns over legal risks and corporate governance in international operations. (Wikipedia)
A US court reduced the penalty in the Epic Systems case to around $420 million, easing financial impact on TCS while still highlighting ongoing legal and compliance challenges. (Wikipedia)
TCS reported steady quarterly earnings supported by strong demand for outsourcing services and increasing adoption of digital technologies across global enterprise clients.
TCS continued stable revenue growth with strong deal pipeline, focusing on digital transformation, cloud services, and automation to maintain leadership in global IT services market.
Tata Sons sold TCS shares worth over $1.25 billion in a bulk deal, reducing its stake while unlocking value and maintaining majority ownership in India’s largest IT services company. (Wikipedia)

TCS reported strong quarterly earnings driven by growth in digital services, including cloud, analytics, and automation, reflecting successful transition from traditional outsourcing to next-generation IT solutions.
TCS delivered consistent earnings growth supported by strong client demand and large deal wins, maintaining its position as a global leader in IT services and consulting.
TCS continued expansion in digital services and enterprise solutions, strengthening its global client base and improving margins through operational efficiency and scalable delivery models.
TCS received multiple international business awards for innovation and service excellence, reinforcing its global brand strength and leadership in IT services and consulting industry. (Wikipedia)

TCS became the world’s most valuable IT company by market capitalization, surpassing Accenture, reflecting strong investor confidence in its digital capabilities and long-term growth prospects. (Wikipedia)
TCS announced operational adjustments during COVID-19, shifting to remote work model while ensuring service continuity and supporting global clients amid widespread business disruptions.
TCS reported resilient earnings despite pandemic challenges, driven by strong demand for digital services and cost optimization initiatives across its global operations.
TCS strengthened its cloud and digital offerings as enterprises accelerated digital transformation during pandemic, increasing demand for IT services, automation, and remote infrastructure solutions.
TCS ended the year with strong deal pipeline and stable margins, positioning itself for continued growth driven by digital transformation, cloud adoption, and global enterprise technology spending.

TCS reported strong quarterly earnings driven by robust demand for digital services, cloud adoption, and large deal wins as global enterprises accelerated technology spending during post-pandemic recovery phase.

TCS announced strong deal pipeline and revenue growth, reflecting continued global demand for IT services and reinforcing its leadership position in digital transformation and enterprise technology solutions.

TCS posted strong quarterly earnings with record deal wins, highlighting sustained demand for cloud, analytics, and digital transformation services across global markets despite macroeconomic uncertainties.
TCS reported highest-ever annual revenue and strong margins, supported by large contracts and expansion in digital services, reinforcing its position as India’s leading IT services exporter.

TCS became title sponsor of the London Marathon, strengthening its global brand presence and reinforcing long-term strategy of building visibility across international markets and communities. (Consultancy.uk)
TCS reported stable earnings with strong deal pipeline but cautious outlook due to global recession concerns, indicating potential slowdown in discretionary technology spending by enterprise clients.

TCS announced CEO transition with K Krithivasan replacing Rajesh Gopinathan, marking a major leadership change aimed at sustaining growth and navigating evolving global technology landscape. (Consultancy.uk)

TCS announced ₹17,000 crore share buyback, returning capital to shareholders while maintaining strong balance sheet and reinforcing confidence in long-term cash flow generation. (Tata Consultancy Services)
TCS reported steady revenue growth with strong deal wins, despite macroeconomic uncertainties, reflecting resilience in global IT spending and continued demand for digital transformation services.
TCS reported moderate growth and margin pressure due to delayed client spending and pricing challenges, highlighting early signs of slowdown in global IT demand environment.

TCS opened new co-innovation hub in London to accelerate digital transformation initiatives and strengthen client collaboration in emerging technologies including AI and cloud solutions. (Consultancy.uk)

TCS secured multiple international deals to enhance digital customer experience platforms, reflecting continued demand for IT modernization services across global financial and retail sectors. (Consultancy.uk)
TCS clarified that its systems were not compromised in a cyberattack affecting client Marks & Spencer, reassuring stakeholders about its cybersecurity resilience and operational integrity. (Reuters)
TCS reported its artificial intelligence business reaching $1.5 billion annualized revenue, highlighting AI as a key growth driver and central pillar of future strategy. (The Times of India)

TCS planned workforce rationalization and operational adjustments amid global slowdown and AI-driven changes, reflecting structural shifts in IT services industry and evolving talent requirements. (The Economic Times)

ICICI Bank expanded its international presence by opening its first branch in Shanghai, strengthening global operations and enhancing cross-border banking capabilities for corporate and trade finance clients. (Wikipedia)

ICICI Bank was impacted by a major debit card data breach affecting millions of customers, leading to large-scale card replacements and raising concerns over cybersecurity in Indian banking systems. (Wikipedia)
Following demonetisation in India, ICICI Bank witnessed surge in deposits and digital transactions as customers shifted toward formal banking channels and electronic payment systems. (Wikipedia)

ICICI Bank reported stable earnings with improving retail loan growth, focusing on strengthening asset quality while managing stress in corporate lending segment during broader banking sector challenges.
ICICI Bank posted moderate quarterly results with pressure on margins and rising NPAs, reflecting ongoing stress in corporate loan portfolio and need for stronger risk management practices.
ICICI Bank continued efforts to improve asset quality and reduce bad loans, focusing on retail lending growth and operational efficiency amid challenges in corporate credit portfolio.
ICICI Bank CEO Chanda Kochhar resigned following allegations of conflict of interest in loan approvals, triggering governance concerns and leadership transition within the bank. (Wikipedia)
Enforcement Directorate attached assets linked to former CEO Chanda Kochhar in loan fraud case, intensifying regulatory scrutiny and reinforcing governance risks for the bank. (Wikipedia)
ICICI Bank initiated leadership stabilization under new CEO Sandeep Bakhshi, focusing on restoring investor confidence, improving governance practices, and strengthening core banking operations.
ICICI Bank reported improved asset quality with declining NPAs and strong retail loan growth, signaling gradual recovery from previous corporate lending stress and governance issues.
ICICI Bank divested stake in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance to raise capital, improving balance sheet strength and focusing on core banking operations. (Wikipedia)
ICICI Bank implemented measures to address COVID-19 disruptions, including loan moratoriums and liquidity support, ensuring continuity of operations during economic uncertainty.
ICICI Bank reported strong quarterly profit growth driven by improved asset quality and retail lending, marking recovery phase after earlier governance and asset quality challenges.
ICICI Bank strengthened digital banking capabilities, expanding mobile and online services to enhance customer experience and support increasing shift toward digital financial transactions.
ICICI Bank ended the year with improved financial performance and stronger balance sheet, reflecting successful turnaround strategy focused on asset quality, governance, and retail banking growth.

ICICI Bank reported strong quarterly earnings with significant improvement in asset quality and profitability, reflecting successful turnaround strategy and continued growth in retail lending and digital banking operations.
ICICI Bank posted robust profit growth driven by higher net interest income and improved asset quality, marking sustained recovery from earlier corporate stress and strengthening investor confidence.
ICICI Bank delivered strong quarterly results with declining NPAs and steady loan growth, supported by retail lending expansion and improved credit quality across its loan portfolio.
ICICI Bank reported strong earnings growth with improving margins and stable asset quality, positioning itself among leading private banks benefiting from economic recovery and rising credit demand.

ICICI Bank posted strong quarterly performance with record profit growth and stable margins, driven by robust retail lending and disciplined underwriting practices across its expanding credit portfolio.
ICICI Bank continued strong growth momentum with rising profits and improving asset quality, benefiting from higher loan growth and efficient cost management in a favorable macroeconomic environment.
ICICI Bank reported strong earnings with improved margins and stable NPAs, reflecting consistent execution strategy and leadership in retail banking and digital transformation initiatives.
ICICI Bank posted steady quarterly earnings with continued improvement in asset quality and loan growth, maintaining strong balance sheet and profitability amid evolving economic conditions.
ICICI Bank delivered consistent profit growth with strong net interest income and stable asset quality, reinforcing its position as one of India’s best-performing private sector banks.

ICICI Bank reported robust earnings with continued growth in retail loans and digital banking services, highlighting strong operational efficiency and sustained demand across consumer and business segments.
RBI imposed penalty of around ₹97.8 lakh on ICICI Bank for regulatory compliance deficiencies, highlighting need for stronger internal controls and adherence to banking regulations. (Enrich Money)
RBI imposed additional penalty of ₹75 lakh on ICICI Bank for compliance breaches, reinforcing regulatory scrutiny despite the bank’s otherwise strong financial performance. (CAalley)

ICICI Bank reduced fixed deposit interest rates following RBI repo rate cut, aligning with monetary policy changes and reflecting broader interest rate transmission across banking sector. (The Economic Times)

ICICI Bank began charging payment aggregators for UPI transactions, signaling shift in digital payment monetization strategy and potential changes in cost structure for digital financial services. (The Economic Times)

RBI imposed penalty of ₹22.73 lakh on ICICI Bank for FEMA-related compliance issues, highlighting continued regulatory oversight and need for strict adherence to foreign exchange regulations. (PSU Connect)
Nifty 50 declined sharply amid global market volatility and concerns over US rate hikes, reflecting sensitivity of Indian equities to global monetary policy and foreign investor flows.

Nifty 50 experienced volatility around Union Budget announcements, with investor sentiment driven by fiscal policy expectations, tax reforms, and infrastructure spending outlook impacting equity markets.

Nifty 50 declined amid global trade war fears between the United States and China, reflecting impact of international geopolitical tensions on emerging market equities including India.

Nifty 50 continued upward momentum driven by global liquidity, retail investor participation, and strong performance of large-cap stocks, especially in technology and financial sectors.
Nifty 50 crossed pre-COVID levels, signaling strong market recovery despite economic contraction, supported by liquidity, stimulus measures, and improving corporate earnings outlook.

Nifty 50 declined sharply amid global market volatility triggered by Russia-Ukraine conflict, reflecting sensitivity of Indian equities to geopolitical risks and foreign investor outflows.

Nifty 50 crossed the 20,000 milestone for the first time, reflecting strong economic growth, corporate earnings expansion, and rising participation from domestic institutional investors. (Wikipedia)
Nifty 50 continued record-breaking rally driven by banking and financial stocks, supported by strong credit growth, improving asset quality, and sustained domestic investor participation.

Nifty 50 ended the year with moderate gains, reflecting slower growth compared to global peers but supported by domestic flows, policy support, and improving corporate earnings outlook. (Reuters)

Global inflation began moderating after peak levels, though remained above central bank targets, requiring cautious policy approach. (OECD)
Global economy showed gradual recovery with stable growth and easing inflation, though geopolitical risks continued to pose challenges. (Deloitte)
Oil price volatility continued due to geopolitical tensions, impacting inflation and macroeconomic stability in oil-importing countries like India. (Union Bank of India)
Global economy ended year with moderate growth and easing inflation, though structural risks from geopolitics and trade tensions remained.
